ontology.foodex2_description: Spinach, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for the spinach-dominant curried vegetable dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Bori Palong Sag. A0FJP (cooked spinach) is the dominant-ingredient anchor. Lentil-ball (bori) component is secondary.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl serving of Bori Palong Sag is approximately 250 g, typical of a side-dish portion served alongside rice in a Bengali or Indian meal.

nutrient_source.title: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — spinach (palak) cooked dish and lentil (masoor/urad dal) composition data; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for spinach (cooked) and lentils
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Bori Palong Sag exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from ICMR-NIN cooked spinach (palak) data and lentil (urad dal bori) composition, combined in approximate proportions (~55% spinach, ~25% lentil balls, ~10% oil, ~10% spices/water). Input values from ai_generated record (confidence 0.7) are consistent with this estimation and have been retained with cross-reference validation. Energy 112 kcal/100g, protein 4.8g, fat 6.0g, carbohydrate 10.0g are plausible for this composition.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — lentils (cooked) GI ~29–32; spinach negligible carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bori Palong Sag. GI estimated at 32 based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor being lentils (bori/urad dal), which have a published GI of 29–32 (cooked lentils, University of Sydney GI Database). Spinach contributes negligible carbohydrate. GL per serving = (32 × 10.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 8.0 g per 250 g bowl.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 220 mg/100 g (550 mg per 250 g serving); moderate sodium load with beneficial potassium from spinach and lentils
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and avoid adding extra salt during cooking, as the 220 mg sodium per 100 g can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium targets (≤2000 mg/day per WHO); spinach and lentils provide potassium that partially offsets sodium impact per DASH diet principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~32), moderate carbohydrate (10 g/100 g), high fibre (2.8 g/100 g), and plant protein (4.8 g/100 g) from lentils slow postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bori Palong Sag is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the low glycaemic index (~32) and moderate fibre content (2.8 g/100 g) support glycaemic control, and a standard 250 g serving delivers a glycaemic load of approximately 8, which is within the low GL range; pair with non-starchy vegetables and avoid high-GI accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, and anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, fenugreek) in panch phoron
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is cardioprotective in profile — saturated fat is low at 1.0 g/100 g, trans fat and cholesterol are absent, and the panch phoron spice blend (including fenugreek and cumin) has evidence-based lipid-modulating properties; sodium at 220 mg/100 g should be monitored in patients with heart failure or sodium-restricted diets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric/curcumin, ginger, fenugreek) in panch phoron; low saturated fat; no trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Generally suitable for rheumatoid arthritis due to anti-inflammatory spice profile; individuals with nightshade sensitivity should monitor response to green chilli; ensure cooking oil is not high-saturated-fat palm oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bori Palong Sag is a traditional Bengali Indian dish combining sun-dried lentil balls (bori, made from urad dal or masoor dal paste) with fresh spinach (palong sag), cooked together in a light curry seasoned with panch phoron — a Bengali five-spice blend of fenugreek, nigella, cumin, black mustard, and fennel seeds. The dish is typically prepared by first frying the bori until golden, then tempering mustard oil with panch phoron and green chilli, adding spinach and simmering until wilted, and finally incorporating the fried lentil balls. The result is a mildly spiced, earthy, and slightly nutty dish with a soft-textured spinach base and chewy lentil dumplings. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 112 kcal per 100 g, with 4.8 g protein, 6.0 g fat, and 10.0 g carbohydrate. The low glycaemic index (~32) and moderate dietary fibre (2.8 g/100 g) make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular risk. Iron (1.6 mg/100 g) and calcium (48 mg/100 g) from spinach and lentils support micronutrient needs. The dish is vegan, vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free. It is commonly served as a side dish with steamed rice in Bengali households and is found in Indian diaspora communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia.
common_user_queries: Is bori palong sag good for diabetes?
How is bori palong sag made?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Bori Palong Sag exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from component-level data (cooked spinach and lentil/urad dal bori) from ICMR-NIN and USDA SR Legacy, cross-validated against the ai_generated input values (confidence 0.7). GI is analogously estimated from published lentil GI data. Confidence is medium due to estimation basis and absence of a direct measured entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this traditional Bengali spinach-lentil preparation is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this specific dish combination is not reported in available approved sources; spinach and lentils are known high-potassium foods but a composite value cannot be confirmed without direct measurement. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite dish are not available in approved sources as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — fully plant-based vegan dish with no animal-derived ingredients). Ethnic tags 'Biryani' and 'Naan' from input have been removed as they are not relevant to this dish. Input source URL (foodo.sg) was not used as a primary nutrient source; ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central were used instead per approved source policy.
